operating_state: Total revenue increased 22% to $47.5 billion driven by advertising revenue growth with ad impressions up 11% and average price per ad up 9% globally. Family of Apps daily active people grew 6% to 3.48 billion while Reality Labs contributed from Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses sales offset by lower Quest sales. Operating cash flows reached $49.6 billion for six months, up from $38.6 billion prior year, reflecting higher revenue generation and lower cash tax payments.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 12% driven by higher infrastructure costs and employee compensation, with research and development rising 23% supporting AI initiatives and Reality Labs investments. Operating margin expanded to 43% from 38% prior year, benefiting from $1.58 billion reduction in depreciation expense due to extended server useful lives to 5.5 years. Reality Labs operating losses of $4.5 billion continued reducing overall profitability despite revenue growth.
material_events: Board increased quarterly dividend 5% to $0.525 per share with share repurchases totaling $22.9 billion for six months. The company completed investment in Scale AI and approved disposal of data center assets for co-development arrangements. European Commission ruled Meta's subscription model violates DMA requirements in April 2025, which Meta has appealed, while board expanded to fifteen members with two new independent directors.
macro_exposures: European regulatory developments significantly impact advertising capabilities, with DMA compliance requiring less personalized ads for users declining subscription options beginning Q3 2025. Foreign currency translation generated $2.8 billion positive impact for six months, while iOS changes and mobile restrictions continue reducing ad targeting capabilities. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act enacted July 2025 will reduce U.S. federal cash tax payments, though global minimum tax regimes could increase effective rates.
forward_outlook: Capital expenditures reached $29.5 billion for six months with management projecting $66-72 billion for full year 2025 to support AI infrastructure and core business investments. Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase in 2025 despite continued substantial investments in metaverse and wearables development. The company maintains $28.2 billion remaining share repurchase authorization and extended tender offers for additional equity interests in unconsolidated entities.
uncertainty_profile: FTC antitrust litigation trial concluded May 2025 with decision expected second half, while multiple youth-related litigation trials scheduled November 2025 present material exposure. Tax Court issued May 2025 opinion requiring $1.48 billion higher intellectual property valuation for 2010 transfer pricing with appeal rights available. Outstanding contractual commitments total $27.95 billion primarily for infrastructure, with maximum exposure to loss in unconsolidated entities at $1.98 billion.
